Data as of: June 14, 2026

Campaign Overview

May 1 – June 14, 2026

📋 Performance Summary

Meta · May 4 – June 14 TikTok · May 11 – June 14 Walmart · May Final + June In Progress Display · May 20 – June 14 Off Story · May 27 – June 14

The Dippin' Dots Spring/Summer 2026 paid media campaign is pacing well through six-plus weeks in market. Data reflects May 1 – June 14, 2026. Walmart's May results are preserved as final historical data due to a platform attribution model update on June 1; new Walmart campaigns are tracked separately.

Meta continues to deliver standout efficiency. The Engagement campaign has generated 18,848 post engagements at a $0.13 CPE with a 16.22% engagement rate — well above benchmarks for the category. The Traffic/Retargeting campaign has driven 6,323 landing page views at just $0.17 per visit with a 3.79% CTR — more than 6× the 0.56% Meta average. Frequency is at 1.69, well within healthy range.

TikTok has now surpassed 1.68M impressions at a sustained $1.79 CPM — less than half the $3–4+ benchmark — confirming strong creative-algorithm fit through six weeks in market.

Walmart May campaigns closed at 2.40× blended ROAS on $20,017 in attributed sales — final results under the previous attribution model. New June campaigns (June 1–14) have generated $5,019 in attributed sales on $4,893 in spend for a 1.03× blended ROAS — Contextual leading at 1.14× ROAS.

Display Custom/Carousel has now generated 4.55M impressions across 16 retail partners (May 20–June 14), with Kroger, Walmart, and Stop & Shop leading on volume. A new Off Story campaign launched May 27 across 11 retail partners, adding 306,139 impressions at a strong 1.23% CTR and 72.3% story completion rate.

Walmart Connect

Sales Campaign · May–Sept 2026
⚠️ Attribution Model Update — June 1: Walmart Connect updated its attribution model on June 1, 2026. New campaigns were built starting June 1 to accommodate. May data is preserved as final historical results (Previous Model). June results are tracked separately below (New Model).
📋 May Final Results — May 12–31, 2026 (Previous Attribution Model) — These numbers are complete and will not be updated. Contextual targeting outperformed behavioral: 3.74× vs. 1.58× ROAS.

💡

Off Story Highlights

Key Takeaways

10× higher CTR than Custom/Carousel (1.23% vs. 0.12%) — story format is driving significantly more click-through behavior.

72.3% story completion rate indicates high creative relevance; users are watching through to the end.

96.2% story start rate confirms strong initial engagement — the creative is capturing attention at the impression stage.

Creative Performance

Meta + TikTok · Full Month
📊 Creative Analysis — May 1–31, 2026 🗓️ Updated Monthly — Next update: end of June 2026 The creatives below are ranked by their primary platform KPI: Engagement Rate & CPE for Meta, and Reach & CPM for TikTok. Top performers should be sustained and used as a template for new creative development. Assets flagged for refresh are underdelivering relative to campaign averages and should be prioritized for replacement.
